title: Pittsburgh's Loudest Instagram Feeds
subtitle: Ten small-business accounts the city is actually showing up for this quarter.
angle: The Pittsburgh small-business Instagram accounts whose audience is genuinely engaged this quarter, illustrated by one recent post each. Ranked, not scored.
intro:
Spot of Coffee runs an account with a starter-pack follower count and a comment section that reads like a group chat. Eight-hundred-something people had something to say about a single closed-on-Mondays giveaway post. That is the kind of audience this list is about.

These are the ten Pittsburgh small-business feeds whose followers actually do something this quarter. They comment. They share. They watch the whole reel. Most of them are not big. Delanie's in Shadyside has a few thousand followers and posted one tagged honky-tonk photo that pulled the kind of likes giveaways usually need a hundred-dollar gift card to generate. Page's, the seventy-five-year-old ice cream stand in Arlington, lands at six on the strength of one ice cream truck reveal that read like a citywide RSVP. Hidden Harbor in Squirrel Hill is here because tiki mugs photograph well and people still respond to a good drink.

National chains are out. So is anything we suspected was being indexed by accident. What's left is a list of operators who know who they're posting for. Issue 02 will widen the pool and start naming the feeds that hold their spot.
